mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-payroll growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and inflation signals strong demand pressures that push equity valuations higher, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL both rise > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or returns in triggered periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles marked by FEDFUNDS above 4.5% compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-FEDFUNDS regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improved supply chain efficiency and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-INDPRO periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction and consumer spending signals strong demand conditions that push equity prices higher, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or returns in triggered periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate below 3.6% signals tight labor markets that support wage growth and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low-UNRATE regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth above 1% signals robust economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high-GDPC1 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.
